metadata.epicTitle: Account Registration Page with Email Verification
metadata.description: Kanban board for Account Registration Page with Email Verification. Contains original epic task (as reference) and all broken-down subtasks (1-2 day units).

tasks.title: Account Registration Page with Email Verification
tasks.description: Create public sign-up page that allows prospective store owners to create accounts with email verification. Includes company/store name collection and initial subscription tier selection.
tasks.userStory: As a prospective store owner, I want to sign up for an account with email verification so that I can securely create my store account and start using the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user visits the sign-up page, when they enter valid email, password, company name, and select a subscription tier, then they receive a verification email and account is created in pending state
Given a user submits the sign-up form, when the email already exists in the system, then they receive an error message indicating the email is already registered
Given a user receives a verification email, when they click the verification link, then their account is activated and they can log in
Given a user submits the sign-up form, when required fields are missing or invalid, then validation errors are displayed for each invalid field
Given a user successfully signs up, when they complete registration, then they are redirected to the onboarding flow
Given a user signs up, when they select a subscription tier, then the 30-day free trial period is automatically activated without requiring payment information
tasks.businessValue: Enables stores to onboard quickly and start using the platform. Foundation for all other features.
tasks.technicalNotes: Requires Azure AD B2C integration for authentication. Email verification should use secure tokens with expiration.
successMetrics: Account sign-up conversion rate: 60%+ of visitors who start sign-up complete it
Email verification rate: 80%+ of sign-ups verify their email within 24 hours
businessRules: Email must be unique across all accounts
Password must meet security requirements (minimum 8 characters, complexity requirements)
Company name is required and must be at least 2 characters
Subscription tier selection is required but can be changed later
30-day free trial starts immediately upon account creation
tasks.note: This is the original epic task. The subtasks below break this down into 1-2 day units.

tasks.title: Frontend Sign-Up Form UI Component
tasks.description: Create React component for account registration form with fields for email, password, company name, and subscription tier selection using Material UI.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want to create the sign-up form UI so that users can enter their registration information in a user-friendly interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the sign-up page, when rendered, then it displays form fields: email, password, confirm password, company name, and subscription tier dropdown
Given form fields, when user interacts, then fields have proper labels, placeholders, and validation feedback
Given the form, when displayed, then it follows Material UI design system and is responsive on mobile and desktop
Given form submission, when user clicks submit, then form validation runs before API call
Given loading state, when form is submitting, then submit button shows loading indicator and is disabled
tasks.businessValue: Provides user interface for account registration, enabling stores to sign up for the platform.
tasks.technicalNotes: Use Material UI TextField, Select, and Button components. Implement form state management with React hooks (useState). Create reusable form components.
successMetrics: Form renders correctly on all screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop)
All form fields are accessible (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance)
businessRules: Form must be accessible (keyboard navigation, screen readers)
Password field must have show/hide toggle
Subscription tier dropdown must show all available tiers with descriptions

tasks.title: Sign-Up Form Validation Logic
tasks.description: Implement client-side and server-side validation for sign-up form including email format, password strength, and required fields.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want to validate sign-up form inputs so that invalid data is caught before submission and users receive clear feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Given email field, when user enters invalid email format, then validation error is displayed immediately with clear message
Given password field, when password doesn't meet requirements, then specific error message shows all requirements (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number)
Given password confirmation, when passwords don't match, then error is displayed with message 'Passwords do not match'
Given company name field, when empty or less than 2 characters, then validation error is shown
Given form submission, when validation fails, then submission is prevented and all errors are displayed
Given server validation, when API returns validation errors, then they are displayed to user with field-level error messages
tasks.businessValue: Ensures data quality and prevents invalid account creation, improving user experience with clear error messages.
tasks.technicalNotes: Use regex for email validation. Implement password strength checker. Validate on both client (immediate feedback) and server (security). Use Material UI error states.
successMetrics: 100% of invalid inputs are caught before submission
Validation errors are clear and actionable (users understand how to fix)
businessRules: Email must be valid format (RFC 5322 compliant)
Password: minimum 8 characters, at least 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number
Company name: 2-100 characters, alphanumeric and spaces allowed
All required fields must be validated on both client and server

tasks.title: Backend API Endpoint for Account Registration
tasks.description: Create Azure Function HTTP endpoint that handles account registration, validates input, creates company record, and initiates email verification.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want a backend endpoint for account registration so that accounts can be created securely with proper validation and data storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Given registration request, when valid data is received, then Company record is created in database with pending status
Given registration request, when email already exists, then 409 Conflict error is returned with message 'Email already registered'
Given company creation, when successful, then 30-day trial period is initialized with start date set to current date
Given registration, when processed, then email verification token is generated (secure, random, expires in 24 hours) and stored
Given registration success, when account is created, then 201 Created response is returned with account ID and status
Given registration, when subscription tier is selected, then tier is associated with company record
tasks.businessValue: Enables secure account creation on the backend with proper data validation and storage.
tasks.technicalNotes: Create RegisterAccount HTTP function. Use SQL parameterization to prevent injection. Generate secure verification tokens (use crypto library). Store in Company table. Use transactions for data consistency.
successMetrics: API endpoint responds in <500ms for valid requests
100% of valid registrations succeed and create company records
businessRules: Email must be unique across all accounts
Company name must be unique per tenant (if multi-tenant)
Verification token expires in 24 hours
Trial start date is set to account creation date
Account status starts as 'Pending' until email verified

tasks.title: Email Verification Service Integration
tasks.description: Integrate email service (SendGrid) to send verification emails with secure verification links.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want to send verification emails so that users can verify their email addresses and activate their accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Given account registration, when account is created, then verification email is queued for sending via SendGrid
Given email service, when email is sent, then it includes verification link with secure token in URL parameter
Given email template, when rendered, then it includes company branding, clear instructions, and verification button/link
Given email sending, when SendGrid service is unavailable, then email is queued for retry with exponential backoff
Given email delivery, when sent successfully, then delivery status is logged for tracking
Given email template, when viewed, then it is mobile-responsive and renders correctly in major email clients
tasks.businessValue: Enables email verification for account security, ensuring users have valid email addresses.
tasks.technicalNotes: Integrate SendGrid API (use SendGrid NuGet package or REST API). Create email template (HTML with plain text fallback). Implement retry logic for failed sends. Store email status in database. Use SendGrid dynamic templates or create custom template.
successMetrics: 95%+ of verification emails are delivered successfully
Emails sent within 30 seconds of account registration
Email delivery rate: 98%+ (accounting for invalid emails)
businessRules: Verification link must be HTTPS
Link expires in 24 hours
Link can only be used once
Email must include unsubscribe option (for compliance)
Verification link format: https://app.domain.com/verify?token={token}

tasks.title: Email Verification Link Handler
tasks.description: Create endpoint and UI to handle email verification link clicks, validate tokens, and activate accounts.
tasks.userStory: As a developer, I want to handle verification link clicks so that accounts can be activated when users verify their email addresses.
acceptanceCriteria: Given verification link, when clicked, then token is extracted from URL and validated for expiration and validity
Given valid token, when verified, then account status is updated from 'Pending' to 'Active' in database
Given invalid token, when accessed, then error page is shown with message and option to resend verification
Given expired token, when accessed, then user is informed token expired and can request new verification email
Given successful verification, when account is activated, then user is redirected to login page with success message
Given verification, when account is already verified, then user is informed account is already active and redirected to login
tasks.businessValue: Completes the email verification flow, enabling users to activate their accounts.
tasks.technicalNotes: Create VerifyEmail HTTP function. Validate token (check expiration, existence, single-use). Update account status in database. Create verification result page (success/error). Handle token reuse attempts.
successMetrics: 100% of valid tokens successfully verify accounts
Invalid/expired tokens handled gracefully with clear error messages
businessRules: Token is single-use (marked as used after verification)
Token expires in 24 hours from generation
Account can only be verified once
Already-verified accounts show appropriate message

tasks.title: Resend Verification Email Functionality
tasks.description: Add ability for users to request a new verification email if original is lost or expired.
tasks.userStory: As a user, I want to request a new verification email so that I can verify my account if I lost the original email or it expired.
acceptanceCriteria: Given unverified account, when user requests resend, then new verification email is sent with new token
Given resend request, when email is sent, then previous token is invalidated and new token is generated
Given resend rate limiting, when user requests more than 3 times per hour, then rate limit error is returned
Given resend, when successful, then confirmation message is displayed to user
Given resend request, when account is already verified, then user is informed account is already active
tasks.businessValue: Improves user experience by allowing verification email recovery, reducing support requests.
tasks.technicalNotes: Create ResendVerification HTTP function. Implement rate limiting (max 3 per hour per email). Invalidate old tokens. Generate new token. Send email using same service as initial verification.
successMetrics: Rate limiting prevents abuse (max 3 per hour enforced)
businessRules: Max 3 resend requests per hour per email address
New token invalidates previous token
Rate limit resets after 1 hour from first request
Already-verified accounts cannot request resend
